## Structure

TCP S→C 0x8317 — **chat / system message** broadcast.
Variable-length body. Verified 2026-05-10 against samples
from 5/17 retail captures (only PvP / Plaza / VEHICLE_DRONE
captures see chat traffic).

```
[0..1]   83 17                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2..5]   sender_uid LE32        speaker's char_uid
                                  OR 0xffffffff for system
                                  /broadcast messages
[6]      name_len u8            length of speaker's name in chars
[7]      channel u8             chat channel (3=team/trade,
                                  4=local-say, 5=zone/drop,
                                  0xff=server broadcast)
[8]      sub_channel u8         0x00 / 0x10 / 0x11 — chat
                                  sub-channel or zone-filter
[9..]    name + message         ASCII concatenated:
                                  first name_len bytes = sender,
                                  remainder = message text
```

Verified samples:

```
83 17  78 86 01 00  0c  04 00  4e6f726d616e2047617465 73686579
        ^char_uid     ^len=12 ^chnl=4 (LOCAL)  "Norman Gates" + "hey"
   LONG_PARTY_A — local chat: <Norman Gates> "hey"

83 17  78 86 01 00  0c  03 00  4e6f726d616e20476174 657348656c6c6f207465616d
                              ^chnl=3 (TEAM)   "Norman Gates" + "Hello team"
   LONG_PARTY_A — team chat: <Norman Gates> "Hello team"

83 17  d2 86 01 00  08  04 00  4472 61204d6f6e69 686579206261636b
                              ^chnl=4         "Dra Moni" + "hey back"

83 17  ff ff ff ff  0c  ff 00  5365 7276657220416 4d696e [NCPD ...
        ^system uid              ^chnl=0xff   "Server Admin" + msg
   VEHICLE_DRONE — server broadcast (NCPD surveillance announcement)

83 17  10 20 00 00  06  05 10  4a 61 66 66 6f 6f 67 65 6c 20 6f 6e 20 66 6c 6f 6f 72
                              ^chnl=5    "Jaffoo"+"gel on floor"
   PLAZA — drop notification (zone channel)

83 17  c9 17 00 00  07  05 11  51756575657a7a6572 4775 74656e204162656e6423
                              ^chnl=5  "Quezzer" + "Guten Abend#"
   CREATION_LEVELING — German "good evening" zone chat
```

## Variants

Single packet form. Body length varies (24-219B in retail)
based on:
- `name_len`: 5-12 characters typical
- message length: from 3 chars ("hey") to 200+ chars
  (server announcements)

The catalog samples show 17-20 distinct messages across the 5
chat-active captures. The wide size range (24..219B) is
purely from message-length variation.

## Observed contexts

Concentrated in social/PvP captures:
- `RETAIL_CREATION_LEVELING_LONG` × 8 (zone chat during levelling)
- `RETAIL_LONG_PARTY_A/B` × 5/5 (team chat in 4-player party)
- `RETAIL_PLAZA_TO_PEPPER_CROSS_DISTRICT` × 1 (drop notification)
- `RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE` × 1 (server broadcast)

The 12 captures that DON'T see this packet are solo-play
sessions where no chat occurred.

Channel-byte distribution observed:
- `0x03`: team chat / trade chat (LONG_PARTY)
- `0x04`: local "say" chat (LONG_PARTY)
- `0x05`: zone-level chat or drop notifications (PLAZA,
  CREATION_LEVELING)
- `0xff`: server broadcast (system messages, NCPD bulletins)

## Open questions

- Is the layout actually `[sender_uid][name_len][channel][sub_channel][name][msg]`,
  or could `name_len` be something else (e.g. message-class
  enum)? The byte values consistently match name length in
  retail samples — strong but not proof.
- The `sub_channel` byte (0x00/0x10/0x11): semantic unclear.
  Possibly chat sub-tier (whisper, party-leader-only, etc.)
  or zone-id filter.
- Why the LE32 sender_uid even for system messages
  (`0xffffffff`)? Just a sentinel for "no sender" vs an actual
  character UID.

## Server-side handler

**Currently emitted** by the Chat subsystem (see
`Chat8317.java` in `server.gameserver.packets.server_tcp`).
Verify the existing implementation matches this 4-channel
breakdown — particularly that channel=0xff for server
broadcasts uses sender_uid=0xffffffff.

For full retail parity:
1. Channel 3 (team) requires routing to all team members
   only.
2. Channel 4 (local say) requires zone-proximity filtering.
3. Channel 5 (zone) is for drop notifications and zone-wide
   chatter.
4. Channel 0xff bypasses filtering — broadcasts to all.

The `name_len` field is critical — modern client requires
exactly `name_len` ASCII bytes for the sender name before the
message starts.
